hypothesis: On MADE configured with DFT oracles for ternary intermetallic systems, a multi-fidelity Bayesian optimization policy that models energy-above-hull using cheap MLIP labels and sparse DFT corrections and selects DFT queries by maximizing expected growth of the convex-hull stable set will discover at least twice as many DFT-stable, Materials Project–novel compounds as a MatterGen plus diversity acquisition baseline under an identical DFT budget of 40 queries.
